commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
New, by their form and qualities, but not by their substance.
The first heaven and first earth was passed away: being changed, not as to their substance, but in their qualities. (Challoner)

commentaries.author: Hippolytus of Rome
commentaries.content: In fornications, in adulteries, and in lies and uncleanness, and in idolatries, and in murders, and in battles. For there shall be the new heaven and the new earth.

commentaries.author: Irenaeus of Lyons
commentaries.content: And He will wipe away every tear from their eyes; and death shall be no more, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain, because the former things have passed away."

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content:
Says He. "The first heaven and the first earth passed away".
Even "the sea shall be no more."

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
